summary: Tested the new Pricing Foundation + Pricing Engine rebuild (backend API + full frontend UI flows). Verified: (1) Settings > Pricing Foundation method selector toggle (banners category) updates active state + JSON preview + persists via Save All (confirmed via GET /api/pricing-foundation), (2) Orders: created order, added banner item, edited specs (width/height/unit/material), Calculate produced correct pricing breakdown ($69.12 = sell_rate_per_sqft, matches engine formula), Override Price flow verified no-op with empty reason and successful override with reason (badge showed 'Manually overridden by preview-user: reason', item price + order total updated to $75.00), (3) Quotes: created quote, added banner line item, Calculate produced breakdown ($35.00 with default specs), Override Price flow verified (badge shown, subtotal/total updated to $42.00), (4) Regression: full Quote->approve->convert-to-order->generate-invoice flow (18/18 pytest passing) and full backend suite (131/137 passing, same 6 pre-existing unrelated failures main agent already flagged: 3 auth-bypass-mode 401 tests + 3 broken generate-quote-draft tests referencing a nonexistent route -- confirmed unrelated to pricing work).
critical_code_review_comments: services/pricing_engine.py is well-structured: single PricingResult contract, clean per-category adapters, FOUNDATION_CATEGORY_ALIASES correctly handles vehicle_wrap<->vehicle_graphics and promo_misc<->promotional legacy naming mismatch (verified both via golden unit tests and UI).

Override provenance (reason/actor/timestamp) correctly implemented in both OrdersRepository.set_pricing_override and QuotesRepository.set_pricing_override -- verified end-to-end in UI (badge text 'Manually overridden by preview-user: "<reason>"').

Frontend override panel correctly no-ops when reason is empty (verified: price stayed unchanged after submit attempt with blank reason) -- good client-side guard preventing accidental audit-trail-less overrides.

Minor test-authoring note (not a bug): the override reason/price inputs in OrdersWorkspace/QuotesWorkspace do not have a 'type' attribute distinguishing them for selectors beyond data-testid, but data-testid coverage is complete and reliable for all pricing/override elements tested.
No _id leakage observed; PUT /api/pricing-foundation, calculate-pricing, override-pricing all return clean JSON.

success_rate.backend: 100% (6/6 new pricing API tests + 13/13 golden formula tests + 18/18 regression tests passed; full suite 131/137 with 6 pre-existing unrelated failures)
success_rate.frontend: 100% of tested pricing flows passed (Settings method selector, Orders calculate+override, Quotes calculate+override)

seed_data_creation: Created TEST_-prefixed order (ORD-0036, TEST_UIOrderCustomer) and quote (QUO-0042, TEST_UIQuoteCustomer) via UI, plus TEST_-prefixed order/quote via backend/tests/test_pricing_api_flows.py (Not cleaned up, left in place per convention). Also changed Pricing Foundation banners.calculation_method to 'sell_rate_per_sqft' via UI Save All (was previously unset/cost_plus) - this is a real settings change, not test pollution, but flagging in case main agent expects a specific default state.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pricing Foundation + Pricing Engine feature fully verified end-to-end at both API and UI layers for the banners category (area-family driver, both calculation methods). Did not exhaustively UI-test every category (vehicle_wrap/services/apparel/promo_misc/custom) beyond the backend golden tests which already cover all of them -- if a future session wants full UI coverage of every category's method selector + calculate + override, that would be a reasonable next step, but banners category proves the pattern works correctly end-to-end (frontend calls correct endpoints, backend engine reads foundation settings, override provenance captured and displayed). No bugs found this session.
